name: Operating Systems
description: Process management, memory management, file systems, and concurrency. Includes lab component with kernel programming exercises.

name: Computer Networks
description: Network protocols, TCP/IP stack, routing, transport layer, and application layer protocols.

name: Data Structures and Algorithms
description: Fundamental data structures, algorithm design paradigms, complexity analysis, and graph algorithms.

name: Database Systems
description: Relational model, SQL, query optimization, transactions, and NoSQL systems.

name: Compilers
description: Lexing, parsing, semantic analysis, IR generation, optimization, and code generation.

name: Introduction to NLP
description: Text preprocessing, language models, sequence tagging, parsing, and neural approaches to NLP tasks.

name: Machine Learning
description: Supervised and unsupervised learning, neural networks, model evaluation, and practical ML workflows.

name: Computer Vision
description: Image formation, feature extraction, object detection, segmentation, and deep learning for vision.

name: Deep Learning
description: Neural network architectures, optimization, CNNs, RNNs, transformers, and generative models.

name: Algorithms
description: Advanced algorithm design, randomized algorithms, approximation algorithms, and computational geometry.

name: Theory of Computation
description: Automata theory, formal languages, computability, and complexity classes.

name: Distributed Systems
description: Distributed computation models, consensus protocols, fault tolerance, and distributed storage.

name: Cloud Computing
description: Virtualization, container orchestration, serverless computing, and cloud-native application design.

name: Technical Communication
description: Academic and professional writing, oral presentations, and scientific communication skills.

name: Linear Algebra
description: Vector spaces, linear transformations, eigenvalues, matrix decompositions, and applications.

name: Probability and Statistics
description: Probability theory, random variables, distributions, hypothesis testing, and statistical inference.

name: Reinforcement Learning
description: MDPs, dynamic programming, model-free methods, policy gradient, and multi-agent RL.
